flashcards.front: What is a relation from set A to set B?
flashcards.back: A relation R from A to B is a subset of the Cartesian product A × B, i.e., R ⊆ A × B.

flashcards.front: Define a reflexive relation with example
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is reflexive if (a,a) ∈ R for every a ∈ A. Example: R = {(1,1), (2,2), (3,3), (1,2)} on A = {1,2,3}.

flashcards.front: Define a symmetric relation with example
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is symmetric if (a,b) ∈ R implies (b,a) ∈ R for all a,b ∈ A. Example: R = {(1,2), (2,1), (2,3), (3,2)}.

flashcards.front: Define a transitive relation with example
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is transitive if (a,b) ∈ R and (b,c) ∈ R implies (a,c) ∈ R for all a,b,c ∈ A. Example: R = {(1,2), (2,3), (1,3)}.

flashcards.front: Define an antisymmetric relation with example
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is antisymmetric if (a,b) ∈ R and (b,a) ∈ R implies a = b for all a,b ∈ A. Example: R = {(1,2), (1,3), (2,3)} on A = {1,2,3}.
A relation R on set A is an equivalence relation if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive. Example: Equality relation on any set.

flashcards.front: What is a partial order relation?
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is a partial order if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive. Example: ≤ relation on integers.

flashcards.front: Define the inverse (converse) of a relation
flashcards.back: The inverse of relation R, denoted R⁻¹, is defined as R⁻¹ = {(b,a) | (a,b) ∈ R}. If R = {(1,2), (2,3)}, then R⁻¹ = {(2,1), (3,2)}.
The composition R ∘ S of relations R from A to B and S from B to C is defined as R ∘ S = {(a,c) | ∃b ∈ B such that (a,b) ∈ R and (b,c) ∈ S}.

flashcards.front: What is the reflexive closure of a relation?
flashcards.back: The reflexive closure of R is R ∪ Δ, where Δ = {(a,a) | a ∈ A} is the diagonal relation. It is the smallest reflexive relation containing R.

flashcards.front: What is the symmetric closure of a relation?
flashcards.back: The symmetric closure of R is R ∪ R⁻¹. It is the smallest symmetric relation containing R.

flashcards.front: What is the transitive closure of a relation?
flashcards.back: The transitive closure of R, denoted R⁺, is the smallest transitive relation containing R. R⁺ = R ∪ R² ∪ R³ ∪ ... = ⋃ₙ₌₁^∞ Rⁿ.
A congruence relation on an algebraic structure is an equivalence relation that preserves the operations of the structure. For integers modulo n, ≡ₙ is a congruence relation.
Given poset (A, ≤), elements a and b are related by the totient relation if there is no element c ∈ A such that a < c < b. It is also called the covering relation.
A relation R on set A is irreflexive if (a,a) ∉ R for every a ∈ A. Example: < relation on integers.
